relationship_to_restored_text: Interpretive contemplative layer aligned by verse. This does not replace the source-audited restored translation.

verses.mystical_translation: YHWH speaks the sacred rhythm of time.
The appointed times are proclaimed as holy gatherings.
Every week bends toward Sabbath of deep rest.
The annual appointed times are proclaimed in season.
Passover remembers liberation between evenings.
Unleavened Bread carries the memory for seven days.
The first day gathers the people into holy cessation.
Seven days of offering end in another holy convocation.
YHWH speaks over the first harvest.
The first sheaf is brought before the harvest is treated as ordinary possession.
The priest waves firstfruits for acceptance.
A complete lamb joins the wave of firstfruits.
Flour, oil, and wine accompany the offering.
The harvest is not eaten before gratitude is brought near.
Seven complete Sabbaths are counted.
The fiftieth day opens into new grain offering.
Two leavened loaves rise as firstfruits from the dwellings.
The loaves are joined by offerings of abundance.
Purification and peace/well-being offerings accompany the feast.
Bread and lambs are waved as holy provision.
The day becomes holy gathering and rest.
Harvest holiness leaves the field's edges for poor and sojourner.
YHWH speaks the seventh-month awakening.
Trumpet memory breaks the month open in holy rest.
The day refuses ordinary labor.
YHWH speaks the Day of Coverings.
The people humble their souls/lives before YHWH.
Work ceases while covering and cleansing are made.
The day demands real participation, not empty distance.
Ordinary work is silenced before communal cleansing.
The command becomes an age-long rhythm.
From evening to evening the people enter deep Sabbath.
YHWH speaks the festival of booths.
Seven days of booths begin in the gathered harvest.
The first day is holy rest.
Seven days lead to an eighth-day solemn gathering.
Each appointed time receives its own offering and day.
Festival offerings do not erase Sabbaths, vows, gifts, or freewill offerings.
Gathered produce becomes seven days of festival rest.
Fruit and branches become instruments of rejoicing before YHWH.
The joy returns every seventh month through generations.
Israel dwells in booths, leaving permanence for remembered dependence.
The booths teach every generation how YHWH carried Israel out of Egypt.
Moses gives the appointed times to Israel.

contemplative_note: Leviticus 23 turns time into a sanctuary: weekly rest, liberation memory, firstfruits, counting, awakening, cleansing, harvest justice, rejoicing, and temporary booths beneath the care of the Liberator.
